content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Load and reconcile checkpoints without network effects, require current authorization, then prove no lost or duplicated state before restart.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Offline Resume Audit model · Theory Offline Resume Audit, name the owner, permission, revision, Go, seed, URL, scope, robots, response, body, tree, record, frontier, goroutine, accessibility, report, cleanup, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Offline Resume Audit precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Offline Resume Audit model · Theory Offline Resume Audit, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory Go team handles case 3285 during an isolated binary, deployment, and restore defense. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove durable-state boundaries, versioned formats, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, and offline audit; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load and reconcile checkpoints without network effects, require current authorization, then prove no lost or duplicated state before restart.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Offline Resume Audit model · Theory Offline Resume Audit, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Successful decoding is sufficient resume validation.

checks.description: The cumulative go portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker // Evidence: crawler-go-offline-resume-audit-1go0x7.

checks.pattern: // Evidence: crawler-go-offline-resume-audit-1go0x7(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_crawler_go_offline_resume_audit_1go0x7\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?checkpointCases < 14)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?recoveredCases < 13 \|\| recoveredCases > checkpointCases)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?crashPoints < 6)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?lostRecords > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using load and reconcile plus specific case evidence.
